metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends on multiple services; a fault in any direct dependency can plausibly propagate to frontend. With no single downstream identified, multiple parallel paths exist (each dependency to frontend). The blast radius remains focused on frontend, but monitoring the key dependencies (cartservice, productcatalogservice, checkoutservice) is prudent due to their central role in e-commerce flows. The topology indicates no unique downstream, which aligns with the log agent not identifying a single suspected downstream.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources agree that the symptom is frontend-related and that there is no concrete log evidence within the observed window. Topology provides a strong structural basis to consider direct frontend dependencies as potential root causes, with currencyservice, cartservice, and productcatalogservice being the most plausible candidates due to their direct coupling to frontend. The Log Agent provides no observable errors, which shifts attention to unobserved root causes in dependencies. The TCB-RCA baseline centers on the symptom frontend, but cannot identify a deeper root cause without log evidence. Given the absence of cross-service error signals and the structural plausibility of the dependencies, the most credible path is an unobserved fault in one of the direct dependencies, with currencyservice ranked highest due to its central role and common patterns of currency-related flows in e-commerce scenarios.
